TextENGNew: ``Treasure hunting!!  There are two fun things about it.  One is obviously finding something adorable, but can you guess the other?!``
``...Is it when you get all sweaty and find nothing, and suddenly realize the precious worth of life and time?``
changeReason: Old adds 'snapping out of it, and going home' which is not present in the Japanese text.

TextENGNew: They say you never get tired when you're having fun, but just where in that slender body was Rena hiding all this energy?
Rummaging through a pile of oversized garbage was hard work in its own right.
It didn't take long before I gave up, spread a picnic sheet on top of a scrapped car, and collapsed onto it spreadeagled.
changeReason: Old omits 根を上げ (gave up/surrendered), which sets up the context that Keiichi quit treasure hunting; new includes this detail.

TextENGNew: ``Nooo, that's not it~! The fun part is looking through everything, wondering what adorable thing you might stumble upon. That's why even if I don't find anything, it was still a lot of fun.``

``Hmm. ...Well, it wasn't boring for me either.  As a result, this barley tea you brought in the thermos tastes amazing!  This is clearly better than the barley tea at my house. How do I put it — it's something about the fragrance.``
``That's just ordinary stuff from the store. You put the tea bag in a kettle and let it brew.``
``Ah, brewing it must be the secret! At home we just throw it in cold water and put it in the fridge.  I suppose you don't get that depth of flavor without applying heat.``
``Oh, that's right! I forgot!``
She snapped her fingers, then left her treasure hunting aside and came back over to me.
She brought out a wrapped parcel from her bag and opened it.
...My goodness, how thoughtful! Cookies! Their uneven shapes meant they were definitely homemade.
``Did you make these yourself?!  I'll gladly have some. Mm, yes, delicious!``
``They're leftovers from yesterday. Dad ate most of them, so there weren't many left. And Rena barely got any either.``
``I see, then I'd better not eat them all by myself. I'll only have half.  You eat some too!  These are exceptional!  Mm, crunch crunch!  The taste, the texture — absolutely extraordinary!!``
"Rena doesn't need any, so you can eat them all, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS07/02/330200034. Seeing you eat them with such a happy face makes both Rena and the cookies really, really happy."
``Is that so? Then I won't hold back at all!!  I could kill!  I would kill a man for just one of these cookies!!  Munch munch munch!!``
``Ahahaha, that's a terrible way to put it, but I'm happy! I am!``
What a lovely, peaceful moment together...
I was right to go along with Rena's treasure hunt.
...If I had spent the whole day shut up in my room reading manga, it would have been an insult to the day itself!
changeReason: Old adds 'Mion's' before comics, attributing ownership not present in the Japanese; new just says 'manga' matching the JPN.

TextENGNew: The sky was gradually taking on a tinge of crimson.
Flocks of birds flew across the sky beneath the high clouds...  Were they heading back to their nests?
Rena was putting away the wrapping. ...It seemed like it was time to head home.
``So, what was the haul today?``
``Hmm, hopes pinned on tomorrow, I think!``
``This pile still goes so much deeper~! I'm sure something will come up.  Rena can tell by the smell, hau~!!``
``Hahahahahaha. If you believe something will come up, I'm sure it will.  Don't lose hope!  Will it be gold, or a hot spring?!  I'm looking forward to it.``
``If I come treasure hunting again tomorrow, will you come with me?``
``Hm?  Me?  Hmm.  If I can have more of your homemade cookies, I might think about it.``
``I haven't got the cookies ready, so I can't tomorrow... If I don't make cookies... you won't come?``
``Huh?  Ah, ahahahaha, don't look at me with those eyes!  O-Okay, fine!  I'll help out, so stop looking at me like that!  It's hurting my glass conscience~!``
``Hee, ahahahahahahahaha! I got Keiichi-kun's help for tomorrow~!  When Keiichi-kun is with me, hau, it's fun even if I don't find anything.  Ahahahaha!``
Rena usually went treasure hunting alone, but having a companion once in a while was probably just as enjoyable.
She gave me a bright, refreshing smile — one she didn't often let show even in club.
After that, we tidied everything up and prepared to leave.
And the moment we climbed up the embankment, a brilliant flash of light suddenly went off!
``Whoa, bright!?``
``Ha-ha-ha! Caught your blissful smiles on camera!``
``T-Tomitake-san! And Takano-san too! Good afternoon~!``
``Hee hee, good afternoon. Jirou-san really has no tact, does he?  Barging in on your date like that.``
``I-It's not a date! Not a d-date, not a date!  Right, Rena?!``
``H-Hauuu.``

``Ha ha ha ha! A boy and a girl alone in a deserted place like this, talking about the future?  No no, that's perfectly fine!  That's exactly how two people deepen their bond, going through moments like these together.  Right, Takano-san?``
``Hee hee.  In the early days, it was so entertaining watching Jirou-san come up with all sorts of strained excuses to invite me out.``
``There was one excuse in particular that was a masterpiece.  Let me see, what was it...``
``W-W-W-Wait, hold on there!!  That was a misunderstanding on my part, so, um!!``
Takano-san ran off laughing, and Tomitake-san chased after her.
Watching them from a distance, they looked every bit like a couple in perfect harmony.
``Those two seem so mismatched at first glance, but they really do get along well.``
``You're right.  Having such completely opposite personalities, they might fit together like the convex and concave pieces of a puzzle.``
``Hau...  Convex and concave.  Hau hau, what do you mean exactly? What do you mean? What do you mean, what do you mean?``
``I-I don't mean it in a weird way!!  Don't blush — you blushing now makes this incredibly awkward!!``
``I wonder, I wonder!! Convex and concave~~ convex concave convex concave~!! ○△凸※◎~!!!``
Ahh, it's no good — Rena's completely broken!
At times like this, the only way to settle things was the usual conclusion: Rena punches me once and sends me flying.
Go ahead, Rena, I'm ready! Finish me off with one of your lightning-fast punches!
And at that very moment, something came flying through the air and clunked Rena square on the head. What was that?!
Rena suddenly choked and fell to her knees, coughing violently in a way that was clearly not normal.
``H-Hey, are you okay?!  Rena, are you all right?!``
I rubbed Rena's back as she kept coughing. ...What on earth had just happened?!
``What's going on?!  Are you all right, Rena-chan?!``
``Hang on.  Can you breathe?!``
``Did you two throw something that hit Rena just now?``
``What?  What are you talking about?``
I definitely saw it. Something came flying through the air and struck Rena in the head.
I had assumed it was something Tomitake-san or Takano-san threw... but there was no way they would do something so cruel.
Then what was it? Don't tell me something fell from the sky and hit her?
``Are you all right, Rena...?!  Do you need a handkerchief?``
``...Th... thank you...  *cough cough*...!``
``Maybe it went down the wrong way?  Do you feel any better?``
``I-I was looking up at the sky and fooling around... when something suddenly flew into my mouth... and I swallowed it...  Uuu...``
``You swallowed it?!  What did you swallow?  Does your stomach hurt or anything?!``
``It doesn't hurt, but... it feels strange...``
``...Hmm. Maybe an insect flew into your mouth and you accidentally swallowed it?``

``If it's bothering you, we should probably make you vomit. There should be a working tap at the old site office over there.  Drink a lot of water, put your finger to the back of your throat, and get it out.``
``That's a good idea!  Rena, lean on my shoulder and let's go!``
``...Huh...?  ...I feel fine all of a sudden...``
``What's wrong?``
``...Mm.  The little rolling feeling in my stomach — it just quietly disappeared.  ...I don't feel it anymore.``
``Are you sure you're all right?  I hope so...``
``...If it's still bothering you, we should take an X-ray and maybe flush out your stomach.  You're sure it's nothing?``
``...Yes.  Yep, I'm completely fine now.  I don't really understand it myself.  ...It just cleared up, just like that.``
``Is that right...?  Just in case, shouldn't we go to the clinic and have Coach check you over?``
``Mm, thank you!@k@vS07/02/330200073. I'll do that if I still feel off tomorrow.@k@vS07/02/330200074. But for now I'm completely fine.@k@vS07/02/330200075. Thank you all for worrying about me.``
changeReason: Old says 'No, I'm good!' (refusal) while JPN うん、ありがと means 'Yeah, thanks!' (gratitude) — different response type, though both occur in context of being told to visit a doctor.

TextENGNew: Since Rena herself said it was nothing, there was no point in pressing the matter further.
We parted from Tomitake-san and the others there, and I walked Rena back to her home.
At first I was worried she might suddenly take a turn for the worse, but as I listened to Rena tell me over and over how much fun today's treasure hunt had been,
that worry gradually faded away, and we ended up making plans to go hunting together again the next day.
``Goodbye, Keiichi-kun.  Thank you for seeing me home!  If club ends early tomorrow, let's go treasure hunting together again!``
``Yeah!  And thank you for the delicious cookies and barley tea today!!``
``They won't be homemade,  but I'll bring some kind of snack tomorrow.  Look forward to it~!``
``Awesome!!  I can't wait!``
Rena was in such high spirits she seemed ready to burst.
As I watched her, I'd already completely forgotten about the fuss over the foreign object from earlier...
